title: Global Media Ramps Up UEFA & Intercontinental Playoff Preview Coverage Ahead of March 26 Semifinals
detail: With 48 hours until the March 26 playoff semifinals, major sports media published comprehensive previews of the 10 concurrent matches. Al Jazeera published an explainer on when and where the UEFA World Cup playoffs would be played and what was at stake for each of the 16 European nations involved. Key narratives: Italy's desperation to avoid a third consecutive World Cup absence; Kosovo as the tournament's lowest-ranked entrant (FIFA 79th) and the symbolic significance of Kosovo vs. Slovakia; Viktor Gyökeres' form at Arsenal ahead of Sweden vs. Ukraine; and the high-altitude context of Bolivia's CONMEBOL intercontinental campaign. FIFA confirmed the Monterrey and Guadalajara intercontinental venues were operationally ready for the March 26–31 playoff window.

title: USMNT Squad Arrives in Atlanta for Pre-World Cup Belgium and Portugal Friendlies
detail: The 27-man USMNT squad assembled in Atlanta for the March camp, with players converging from club duties across Europe and MLS. Mercedes-Benz Stadium was confirmed as the venue for both the March 28 Belgium friendly (kickoff 3:30 PM ET) and March 31 Portugal friendly (kickoff 7:00 PM ET). The squad was set to debut new Nike 'Stars and Stripes' kits. Belgium (FIFA ranked 9th) and Portugal (FIFA ranked 6th) represent the two highest-quality opponents the USMNT has faced in pre-tournament preparation. Coach Pochettino held the camp's first full training session. Center back depth — flagged as a key concern after the absence of Sergino Dest through injury — was a primary evaluation focus.
